exercises.title: Basic if Statement
exercises.description: An if statement runs a block of code only when the condition is True. Print a warning if the temperature exceeds the threshold.
exercises.starterCode:
temperature = 38.5

# TODO: If temperature > 37.5, print 'WARNING: Fever detected!'
# After the if block (not indented), print 'Check complete.'
if temperature > 37.5:
    print("WARNING: Fever detected!")

print("Check complete.")
hints: The body of the if block must be indented 4 spaces.
The final print() is NOT indented — it runs regardless.

exercises.title: Indentation Defines the Block
exercises.description: Only indented lines are inside the if block. Verify this by seeing which print statements run based on the condition.
exercises.starterCode:
score = 85

if score >= 60:
    print("You passed!")         # Inside if block
    print("Congratulations!")    # Also inside if block

print("End of check.")            # Always runs (not indented)
exercises.expectedOutput:
You passed!
Congratulations!
End of check.
hints: Lines with 4 spaces of indentation are inside the if block.
Lines without extra indentation run regardless of the condition.

exercises.title: Multiple Independent if Statements
exercises.description: Multiple if statements are independent — each is checked separately. Print messages for each condition that applies to the given score.
exercises.expectedOutput:
Above minimum passing score
Above average
Final score: 75
hints: Unlike elif chains, independent if statements each check separately.
All conditions that are True will trigger their blocks.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: if Statement Basics
warmup.description: An if block only runs when the condition is True. What does this print?
